creator: dennis.noordsij@alumni.helsinki.fi
text:
Long debugging story short:

if "virtio_random.ko" is loaded this happens. If it is not loaded, this does not happen.

I do not have virtio-anything loaded on my other KVM VPS'es so this might be the reason I haven't seen it.

Also without loading if_wg.ko at all, unloading virtio_random.ko hangs as well, which it probably shouldn't.


Virtio related dmesg:

virtio_pci0: <VirtIO PCI (modern) GPU adapter> mem 0xfb800000-0xfbffffff,0xfd200000-0xfd203fff,0xfea10000-0xfea10fff irq 21 at device 1.0 on pci0
virtio_pci1: <VirtIO PCI (modern) Network adapter> mem 0xfe880000-0xfe880fff,0xfd000000-0xfd003fff irq 22 at device 0.0 on pci1
vtnet0: <VirtIO Networking Adapter> on virtio_pci1
virtio_pci2: <VirtIO PCI (modern) Console adapter> mem 0xfe400000-0xfe400fff,0xfcc00000-0xfcc03fff irq 22 at device 0.0 on pci3
virtio_pci3: <VirtIO PCI (modern) Balloon adapter> mem 0xfca00000-0xfca03fff irq 22 at device 0.0 on pci4
vtballoon0: <VirtIO Balloon Adapter> on virtio_pci3
virtio_pci4: <VirtIO PCI (modern) Entropy adapter> mem 0xfc800000-0xfc803fff irq 22 at device 0.0 on pci5
virtio_pci5: <VirtIO PCI (modern) SCSI adapter> mem 0xfde00000-0xfde00fff,0xfc600000-0xfc603fff irq 22 at device 0.0 on pci6
vtscsi0: <VirtIO SCSI Adapter> on virtio_pci5
virtio_pci6: <VirtIO PCI (modern) Network adapter> mem 0xfdc80000-0xfdc80fff,0xfc400000-0xfc403fff irq 22 at device 0.0 on pci7
vtnet1: <VirtIO Networking Adapter> on virtio_pci6
vtcon0: <VirtIO Console Adapter> on virtio_pci2
vtrnd0: <VirtIO Entropy Adapter> on virtio_pci4




I would the real issue is not in wireguard-kmod but in virtio? I don't really know what to look for in DDB.

Can someone redirect this to the right place, or suggest the next step?
